summary: Matched two visual/cutscene functions in src/melee/vi/vi1201v1.c, replacing placeholder stubs for fn_8031FC30 and fn_8031FCBC and tightening their header prototypes. The matched code drives a camera object animation, triggers frame-based visual/cutscene events, calls cleanup/transition routines at animation end, and unlinks a GObj after a 30-frame counter threshold. Review discussion emphasized avoiding unnecessary ad-hoc externs, preferring proper headers or split correction, using m2c before AI one-shot attempts, reverting accidental dump artifacts, and using project-style bool literals.
what_changed: Added baselib includes needed by the new matches in vi1201v1.c: <baselib/aobj.h>, <baselib/cobj.h>, and <baselib/gobjplink.h>.

Implemented fn_8031FC30(HSD_GObj* gobj). It obtains the HSD_CObj with GET_COBJ, advances camera animation via HSD_CObjAnim, checks cobj->aobj->curr_frame against 1.0F, 30.0F, and end_frame, then triggers vi_8031C9B4(0xD, 0), un_8031F9D8(un_804D6FFC, un_804D6FFD), and finally lb_800145F4() plus gm_801A4B60().

Implemented fn_8031FCBC(HSD_GObj* gobj). It compares global counter un_804D6FF8 as f32 against 30.0F, unlinks the GObj with HSD_GObjPLink_80390228 once the threshold is reached, otherwise increments the counter.
Updated vi1201v1.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures: un_8031F9D8(u8, u8), fn_8031FC30(HSD_GObj*), and fn_8031FCBC(HSD_GObj*).

The final changed-file list only includes vi1201v1.c and vi1201v1.h, although review excerpts mention earlier or intermediate hunks in vi1101.c, vi1202.c, and orig/GALE01/sys/.gitkeep that appear not to be part of the final diff slice.
smart_moves: Converted unknown function declarations into typed prototypes as soon as call evidence was available, which helps later matches avoid UNK_PARAMS propagation.
Used known HSD access macros and structs, especially GET_COBJ and HSD_CObj->aobj fields, instead of raw object offsets for the matched camera-animation path.
Modeled animation behavior using explicit frame sentinels: first frame, frame 30, and end_frame. This captures cutscene timing in readable C while preserving match-relevant float comparisons.
For the short lifetime/countdown function, preserved the apparent signed/global counter behavior and only calls HSD_GObjPLink_80390228 after the 30-frame threshold.
decomp_lessons: When matching VI/cutscene animation callbacks, inspect HSD_CObjAnim and HSD_AObj frame fields; many actions are keyed directly off curr_frame values and end_frame.
Do not leave newly understood local functions as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in headers. Concrete HSD_GObj* and primitive parameter types improve both matching and cross-TU readability.

Review feedback says local extern declarations are usually avoidable. Prefer including or creating the relevant header, and consider whether a symbol actually belongs to the current translation unit when only referenced in that file.
For AI-assisted matching, reviewers questioned whether m2c was tried. The author noted they would adjust workflow so Claude always attempts m2c first, even if it thinks it can one-shot a function.
Avoid committing accidental source-dump artifacts such as orig/GALE01/sys/.gitkeep; reviewer explicitly requested revert.
Use project-preferred C bool literals true and false instead of TRUE and FALSE when touching bool-returning code, per review feedback in related intermediate hunks.
naming_conventions: Matched functions kept address-derived temporary names fn_8031FC30 and fn_8031FCBC because no semantic names were established in the PR evidence.
OnEnter/OnFrame suffixes remain in nearby declarations such as un_8031FD18_OnEnter and un_80320490_OnFrame, reflecting existing callback naming style.
HSD object callback arguments should be typed as HSD_GObj* when the function is installed or used as a GObj process/callback.
Header comments retain address tags such as /* 31FC30 */ next to declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use m2c as a first-pass aid for decomp attempts before relying on AI-generated source; this was explicitly discussed in review.
Include specific baselib headers for struct field access: cobj/aobj access required <baselib/cobj.h> and <baselib/aobj.h>, while unlinking a GObj required <baselib/gobjplink.h>.

Frame comparisons were expressed as exact float constants, e.g. curr_frame == 1.0F, curr_frame == 30.0F, and curr_frame == cobj->aobj->end_frame, which is likely important for matching generated floating-point compare code.
For fn_8031FCBC, the global integer counter is cast to f32 for comparison against 30.0F before deciding whether to unlink the GObj; preserve this shape if reusing the tactic.
Replacing raw UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete signatures can be part of the match, not just cleanup, because call argument types influence generated code and include requirements.
review_feedback: ribbanya on an intermediate vi1101.c hunk: "Did Claude do this? Did you try using m2c at all?"
itsgrimetime replied that Claude sometimes tries its own first pass before creating a scratch and they would adjust it to always attempt m2c first even if it thinks it can one-shot the function.

ribbanya on vi1201v1.c externs: these externs are not the worst thing, but are never necessary; prefer including the relevant header or creating it, and consider whether symbols belong in the current translation unit when only referenced from the file.
ribbanya on an intermediate vi1202.c hunk: "Just change the argument type."
ribbanya on an intermediate vi1202.c hunk: use true and false, not TRUE and FALSE.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename fn_8031FC30 or fn_8031FCBC to semantic VI/cutscene names.
Search for un_804D6FFC, un_804D6FFD, and un_804D6FF8 to determine whether reviewer feedback about moving externs into a header or current TU was later addressed.
Search VI cutscene files for similar HSD_CObjAnim curr_frame/end_frame patterns that can reuse the fn_8031FC30 matching tactic.
Check later vi1201v1.h edits for further replacement of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations near un_8031FD18_OnEnter and fn_8031FAA8.
Review subsequent author PRs for evidence that the workflow changed to always run m2c before AI source attempts.
